info.title: opencode HttpApi
info.description: Experimental HttpApi surface for selected instance routes.

get.description: Check whether the API server is ready to accept requests.
Resolve the requested location or the server default location.
Retrieve currently registered agents.
Retrieve currently loaded plugins.

schema.description: Maximum number of sessions to return. Defaults to the newest 50 sessions.
Session order for the first page. Use desc for newest first or asc for oldest first.

anyOf.description: Filter by parent session. Use null to return only root sessions.
Opaque pagination cursor returned as cursor.previous or cursor.next in the previous response.

get.description: Retrieve sessions in the requested order. Items keep that order across pages; use cursor.next or cursor.previous to move through the ordered list.

post.description: Create a session at the requested location.

get.description: Retrieve foreground Session drains currently owned by this OpenCode process. Sessions absent from the result are inactive.
Retrieve a session by ID.

delete.description: Delete a session and its child sessions.

post.description: Create a child session by copying projected history from the parent. When messageID is supplied, copy messages before that boundary.
Switch the agent used by subsequent provider turns.
Switch the model used by subsequent provider turns.
Update the session title.
Move a session to another project directory, optionally transferring local changes.
Durably admit one session input and schedule agent-loop execution unless resume is false.
Resolve a slash command into prompt input, admit it durably, and schedule execution unless resume is false.
Activate a skill for a session by appending a skill message and resuming execution.
Append a synthetic message to a session and resume execution.
Execute one shell command in the session's working directory. Emits a shell.started event before execution and a shell.ended event with the merged output after.
Queue a durable session compaction request.
Wait for a session agent loop to become idle.
Stage or move a reversible session boundary and optionally apply its file changes.

get.description: Retrieve the active context messages for a session (all messages after the last compaction).
List API-managed instruction entries attached to the session.

put.description: Attach or replace one durable instruction entry. Changes announce as updates at the next step boundary.

delete.description: Remove one instruction entry; the removal is announced to the model at the next step boundary.

get.description: Experimental durable session event log. Reads events after an exclusive aggregate sequence and continues with live events when follow=true.

post.description: Interrupt active execution owned by this OpenCode process. Idle interruption is a no-op.
Move active foreground backgroundable tools for this session into background observation. Idle requests are a no-op.

get.description: Retrieve one projected message owned by the Session.

schema.description: Maximum number of messages to return. When omitted, the endpoint returns its default page size.
Message order for the first page. Use desc for newest first or asc for oldest first.

anyOf.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned as cursor.previous or cursor.next in the previous response. Do not combine with order.

get.description: Retrieve projected messages for a session. Items keep the requested order across pages; use cursor.next or cursor.previous to move through the ordered timeline.
Retrieve available models ordered by release date.
Retrieve the model used when a session has no explicit model selection.

post.description: Run one stateless model generation at the requested location and return the assistant text. Uses the location's default model when none is specified.

get.description: Retrieve active AI providers so clients can show provider availability and configuration.
Retrieve a single AI provider so clients can inspect its availability and endpoint settings.
Retrieve available integrations and their authentication methods.
Retrieve one integration and its authentication methods.

post.description: Run a key authentication method and store the resulting credential.
Start an OAuth attempt and return the authorization details.

get.description: Poll the current status of an OAuth attempt.

delete.description: Cancel an OAuth attempt and release its resources.

post.description: Complete a code-based OAuth attempt and store the resulting credential.

get.description: Retrieve configured MCP servers and their connection status.
Retrieve resources and resource templates from connected MCP servers.
List known projects.
Resolve the project for the requested location.
List known local absolute directories for a project.
Retrieve pending forms for a location.
Retrieve pending forms for a session.

post.description: Create a form for a session.

get.description: Retrieve a form for a session.
Retrieve the current state for a form.

post.description: Submit an answer to a pending form.
Cancel a pending form.

get.description: Retrieve pending permission requests for a location.
Retrieve saved permissions, optionally filtered by project.

delete.description: Remove a saved permission by ID.

post.description: Evaluate and, when approval is required, create a permission request for a session.

get.description: Retrieve pending permission requests owned by a session.
Retrieve a pending permission request owned by a session.

post.description: Respond to a pending permission request owned by a session.

get.description: Serve one file relative to the requested location.
List direct children of one directory relative to the requested location.
Find recursively ranked filesystem entries relative to the requested location.
Retrieve currently registered commands.
Retrieve currently registered skills.
Subscribe to native event payloads for the server. Volatile by contract: a slow consumer overflows and fails the stream, and events during disconnection are missed.
List PTY sessions for a location, including exited sessions retained until removal.

post.description: Create a pseudo-terminal session for a location.

get.description: Get one PTY session, including its exit code once exited.

put.description: Update the title or viewport size of one PTY session.

delete.description: Terminate and remove one PTY session.

post.description: Create a short-lived single-use ticket for opening a PTY WebSocket connection.

get.description: Establish a WebSocket connection streaming PTY output and accepting terminal input.
List currently running shell commands for a location. Exited commands are not included.

post.description: Spawn one non-interactive shell command for a location. Combined stdout/stderr is captured to a file pageable via output.

get.description: Get one shell command, including its status and exit code once exited.

delete.description: Terminate and remove one shell command and its retained output.

patch.description: Replace a running shell command's timeout from now, or clear it with zero.

get.description: Page through captured combined output by absolute byte cursor.
Retrieve pending question requests for a location.
Retrieve pending question requests owned by a session.

post.description: Answer a pending question request owned by a session.
Reject a pending question request owned by a session.

get.description: List references available in the requested location.
List uncommitted working-copy changes relative to the requested location.
Diff the working copy against HEAD (mode git) or the default-branch merge base (mode branch) for the requested location.
List locations currently loaded by the server.

delete.description: Dispose the requested location's cached services so its next use boots them fresh.

EventLog.Synced.description: Marker emitted once when a log read reaches its captured watermark. The reader holds every event committed at or below seq.

start.description: Startup script to run when creating a new workspace (worktree)

status.description: Current status of the task: pending, in_progress, completed, cancelled

priority.description: Priority level of the task: high, medium, low

custom.description: Allow typing a custom answer (default: true)

answers.description: User answers in order of questions (each answer is an array of selected labels)
